medical_history.chief_complaint: left hemithoracic pain, weight loss, gait ataxia, and tremor
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A 65-year-old man presented to the emergency department with a history of left hemithoracic pain, weight loss, gait ataxia, and tremor. He had a 120-pack-year smoking history and known ischemic cardiomyopathy with previous coronary stenting.
medical_history.past_medical_history: known ischemic cardiomyopathy with previous coronary stenting

physical_examination.specialty_examination: neurological assessment revealed tremor, gait ataxia, and mild involuntary limb jerking which resulted in the patient remaining bedbound despite any evidence of cognitive impairment and sensory or motor deficit

laboratory_examinations.findings: Normal cell counts, protein levels, viral markers, and negative cytology
Anti-Hu, Anti-Ri, anti-Yo, anti-CV2, anti-Ma1, anti-Ma2, anti-amphiphysin, and anti-VGKC were all negative

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Serum glutamic acid decarboxylase antibody radio immunoassay (GAD65 Ab Assay)

radiographic_examinations.findings: Left hilar mass measuring 26 mm with associated left hilar and mediastinal lymphadenopathy (>1 cm short axis)
Staged the SCLC as limited disease

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain
radiographic_examinations.findings: No ischemic changes, metastatic lesions, or abnormal enhancement with gadolinium
Diffuse cerebral hypometabolism with cerebellar hypermetabolism (usually reported finding in PND)

other_tests.procedure_name: Endobronchial ultrasound (EBUS) and transbronchial needle aspiration (TBNA)
other_tests.findings: Confirmed a diagnosis of small-cell lung cancer (SCLC)

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Opsoclonus-myoclonus syndrome (OMS) secondary to small-cell lung cancer (SCLC)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by the patient's symptoms of multivectorial eye movements, involuntary multifocal myoclonus, and cerebellar ataxia, along with the presence of a left hilar mass confirmed as SCLC via endobronchial ultrasound and transbronchial needle aspiration. Brain 18-FDG-PET showed diffuse cerebral hypometabolism with cerebellar hypermetabolism, a reported finding in paraneoplastic neurologic disorders (PND). Although standard serological screening for anti-neuronal antibodies was negative, positive anti-GAD antibodies further supported the diagnosis.
